lemma_statement.name: restricted_continuation_trace_pairing_discharge_lemma
lemma_statement.claim: On the restricted-continuation boundary portion partial Sigma_R, the scalar boundary trace and normal trace admit a certified trace pairing whenever the explicitly listed trace-space compatibility, duality, and boundary-measure hypotheses are supplied.

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_boundary_trace_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the scalar boundary trace object on partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: restricted_continuation_normal_trace_exists
required_inputs.role: supplies the normal trace object on partial Sigma_R

required_inputs.name: boundary_trace_space_duality_compatible
required_inputs.role: places the scalar boundary trace and normal trace in dual-compatible trace spaces

required_inputs.name: trace_pairing_operator_available
required_inputs.role: supplies the pairing map between the certified trace spaces

required_inputs.name: boundary_measure_orientation_compatible
required_inputs.role: ensures the pairing is taken over the same oriented boundary portion partial Sigma_R

discharged_obligations: Separates trace pairing from boundary trace existence.
Separates trace pairing from normal trace existence.
Certifies the exact assumptions under which the restricted-continuation trace pairing is well-defined.
Promotes trace pairing from an undischarged target obligation to a conditional discharge lemma.
non_claims: Does not prove boundary flux pairing.
Does not prove integration by parts.
Does not identify the trace pairing with the physical boundary flux term.
Does not prove flux sign control.
Does not prove full RR closure.
